title: i18n-rosetta Language Card
description: Complete reference for a single language's translation configuration, including metadata, formality system, register presets, method support, and eval dataset links.

code.description: Locale code used as the key in rosetta config. Usually ISO 639-1 (e.g., 'fr') or BCP 47 with region (e.g., 'pt-PT'). For conlangs, use 'x-' prefix.

name.description: English display name of the language.

nativeName.description: Name of the language in its own script. Null for conlangs without established endonyms.

iso639_1.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter code. Null if no ISO 639-1 code exists (e.g., conlangs, some indigenous languages).

iso639_3.description: ISO 639-3 three-letter code. Null for conlangs.

bcp47.description: BCP 47 language tag. The canonical identifier used by web standards.

script.description: ISO 15924 script code (e.g., 'Latn', 'Arab', 'Kore', 'Jpan'). For conlangs using PUA, use the source script code.

formality.description: Structured description of the language's formality system. Null for languages with no formal/informal distinction relevant to translation.

system.description: Category of formality system. Examples: 'T-V' (French, German), 'speech-levels' (Korean), 'keigo' (Japanese), 'particles-and-pronouns' (Thai), 'pronoun-system' (Vietnamese), 'register-levels' (Arabic), 'none'.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of how formality works in this language, written for a developer who doesn't speak it.

default.description: The register preset key to use by default. Must match a key in the registers object.

gender.description: Grammatical gender information. Null for languages with no gender considerations.

grammatical.description: Whether the language has grammatical gender (e.g., French, German, Arabic) vs. no grammatical gender (e.g., Finnish, Korean, Turkish).

inclusiveGuidance.description: Guidance for gender-inclusive language. Injected into all register prompts. Null if not applicable.

registers.description: Named register presets specific to this language's formality system. Keys are preset identifiers (e.g., 'formal-vous', 'polite-haeyo'). Every language must have at least one preset.

label.description: Short display label shown in the CLI wizard and status output.

description.description: One-sentence explanation of when to use this preset.

prompt.description: The actual register instruction injected into the LLM system prompt. This is the text that steers translation tone.

deeplFormality.description: DeepL API formality value for this preset. Only relevant when methodSupport.deeplFormality is true. 'prefer_more' for formal, 'prefer_less' for casual, 'default' for neutral.

aliases.description: Alternative locale codes that should resolve to this language card (e.g., 'no' for 'nb', 'iw' for 'he').

methodSupport.description: Which translation APIs/methods support this language.

deeplFormality.description: Whether DeepL supports the formality parameter for this language. When true, the DeepL method sends prefer_more/prefer_less based on the active register preset.

scriptConverter.description: Key in SCRIPT_CONVERTERS registry (from scripts.js) if this language has a deterministic script conversion step. Null if not applicable.

evalDatasets.description: IDs of evaluation datasets that cover this language (e.g., 'flores-plus-devtest').

notes.description: Free-form notes for developers and contributors.
